steps.title: Identify the independent clauses needing separation
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses: “The vessel took six days to dislodge” (main fact) and “it is as heavy as two thousand blue whales” (elaboration of why).

Between them sits “in part due to its sheer size,” which explains why dislodging was so difficult.

These two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone—they need punctuation that signals the explanatory relationship.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an explanation, elaboration, or reason. In choice B, the colon properly marks the transition to the elaborating fact: the vessel's extreme weight accounts for the six-day dislodging effort.

Choices A and C separate two independent clauses with only a comma (comma splice).

Choice D provides no punctuation between the clauses. That matches choice B — B is correct.
